chloride_mg.note: CoFID: Beef, lean, raw - chloride ~66 mg/100g. Typical muscle chloride range 50-80 mg.

phenylalanine_plus_tyrosine_g.note: Computed: phenylalanine_g(0.867) + tyrosine_g(0.833, estimated ~4.0% of protein).

vitamin_d3_iu.note: Beef muscle raw vitamin D3 minimal (~0.1 IU/100g). Not a meaningful dietary source.

biotin_ug.note: CoFID (UK): Beef, muscle, raw - biotin ~1.5 μg/100g. Muscle meat has low biotin.

vitamin_k_ug.note: CoFID: Beef, raw muscle - vitamin K negligible (~0.1 μg/100g). Not meaningful source.

vitamin_e_iu.note: CoFID: Beef, muscle, raw - vitamin E ~0.12-0.15 IU/100g. Low in all muscle meats.

methionine_plus_cystine_g.note: Computed: methionine_g(0.479, measured) + cystine_g(0.258, estimated ~1.25% of protein from USDA SR Legacy beef).

iodine_ug.note: Median from Milagres2020. Range 0.5-7.0 μg. LOW confidence due to geographic variability.

vitamin_a_iu.note: Beef muscle has negligible vitamin A (trace only). Liver is the vitamin A source.

chloride_mg.note: Connective tissue chloride estimated ~35 mg/100g. Low electrolyte content.

choline_mg.note: Connective tissue choline estimated ~2 mg/100g. Very low.

ara_arachidonic_acid_g.note: Tendon/connective tissue minimal arachidonic acid ~0.01g/100g. Low fat content.

vitamin_d3_iu.note: Connective tissue has no vitamin D3.

biotin_ug.note: Connective tissue/tendon biotin estimated ~0.5 μg/100g. Very low - predominantly collagen.

vitamin_k_ug.note: Connective tissue has negligible vitamin K.

vitamin_e_iu.note: Connective tissue has no vitamin E.

methionine_plus_cystine_g.note: Computed: methionine(0.075) + cystine(0.132, estimated). Collagen has unique AA profile: low Met, high Gly.

iodine_ug.note: Tendon/connective tissue minimal iodine ~0.5 μg/100g. Iodine partitions to aqueous organs.

vitamin_a_iu.note: Tendon/connective tissue has no vitamin A.

chloride_mg.note: CoFID: Blood, raw - chloride ~290 mg/100g. Blood plasma has high chloride concentration. Verified with physiology data.

pyridoxine_b6_mg.note: Blood pyridoxine estimated ~0.05 mg/100g. Very low.

phenylalanine_plus_tyrosine_g.note: Fix: composite must be ≥ phenylalanine_g=1.40; tyrosine not tracked separately in DB

choline_mg.note: Blood free choline low ~2 mg/100g. NOTE: existing DB has choline_mg=0.333 measured; verify if this is total or free choline.

vitamin_d3_iu.reason: sourced from supplement/fortification; excluded from as_fed matrix per formulation_rules

biotin_ug.note: Blood biotin estimated very low (~1.5 μg/100g). No direct DB measurement. Water-soluble vitamin in aqueous phase.

epa_plus_dha_g.reason: Blood is aqueous; contains negligible EPA+DHA. Fat content only ~0.2g/100g with minimal LC-PUFA.

riboflavin_b2_mg.note: Blood riboflavin estimated ~0.1 mg/100g. Very low.

vitamin_e_iu.reason: sourced from supplement/fortification; excluded from as_fed matrix per formulation_rules

methionine_plus_cystine_g.note: Computed: methionine(0.278) + cystine(0.250, estimated ~1.5% of protein). Blood protein is mainly albumin/hemoglobin.

iodine_ug.note: Estimated: blood is iodine transport medium. No direct DB measurement. Plasma iodide ~3 μg/100g.

vitamin_a_iu.reason: sourced from supplement/fortification; excluded from as_fed matrix per formulation_rules

folic_acid_b9_ug.note: Blood folate estimated ~5 μg/100g. No direct USDA FDC measurement. RBC folate is intracellular, whole blood moderate.

pantothenic_acid_b5_mg.note: Blood pantothenic acid estimated ~0.3 mg/100g. Low in whole blood.

ala_alpha_linolenic_acid_g.reason: Blood is aqueous tissue; total fat ~0.2g/100g with negligible ALA content.
